extractions.problem: Whether CD4+ T cells can acquire memory or regulatory phenotypes without antigen encounter, challenging the classical model of antigen-driven T cell maturation.
extractions.method: TCR sequencing and repertoire analysis of highly purified CD4+ T cell populations (naïve, regulatory, central memory, effector memory) from naïve mice, using HEL as model antigen.
extractions.finding: Antigen-inexperienced CD4+ T cells reside in naïve, regulatory, and memory compartments at similar frequencies without clonal expansion, and pathogenic clones are repetitively regenerated throughout adulthood, arguing against T cell-depleting therapies for autoimmunity.
extractions.dataset: Mouse CD4+ T cells, HEL-specific clones, aged vs young mice
extractions.metric: Clone frequency across T cell compartments, TCR sequence identity
extractions.result: HEL-inexperienced clones found at similar frequencies across all four T cell compartments; majority unexpanded

extractions.problem: Developing a predictive model for antiestrogenic activity and discovering novel MCF-7 breast cancer cell line inhibitors.
extractions.method: 3D-QSAR model using pharmacophore-based alignment (Schrödinger Phase), followed by virtual screening of 3D databases and in vitro validation against MCF-7 cells.
extractions.finding: A 5-feature pharmacophore yielded a predictive 3D-QSAR model, and 11 of 14 virtually screened compounds showed inhibitory activity against MCF-7 cells.
extractions.dataset: MCF-7 human breast adenocarcinoma cell line, external test set of 21 compounds
extractions.result: Test set q²=0.73, external r²=0.69, 11/14 hits active with IC50 30-186 μM

extractions.problem: Developing a specific small-molecule inhibitor of Rac GTPase to selectively block its activation by GEF without affecting related GTPases Cdc42 or RhoA.
extractions.method: Structure-based virtual screening targeting a Rac1 surface groove critical for GEF specification, followed by in vitro binding/activation assays and cell-based functional studies.
extractions.finding: NSC23766 selectively inhibits Rac1 activation by Trio/Tiam1 in a dose-dependent manner without affecting Cdc42 or RhoA, and suppresses proliferation, anchorage-independent growth, and invasion in prostate cancer PC-3 cells.
extractions.dataset: Rac1 GTPase, human prostate cancer PC-3 cells
extractions.metric: Dose-dependent inhibition, cell proliferation, anchorage-independent growth, invasion
extractions.baseline: Closely related GTPases Cdc42 and RhoA (selectivity controls)
extractions.result: Selective Rac1 inhibition; no interference with Cdc42/RhoA binding or activation

extractions.problem: How to empower biomedical discovery by designing AI agent systems capable of skeptical learning and reasoning that integrate AI models with experimental platforms.
extractions.method: Perspective/review proposing collaborative AI agents using large language models, generative models, structured memory, and ML tools for biomedical research workflows.
extractions.finding: AI agents can impact virtual cell simulation, phenotype control, cellular circuit design, and therapy development by combining human creativity with AI's analytical capabilities.

extractions.problem: Reducing complexity in programming heterogeneous manufacturing equipment by creating a unified programming platform.
extractions.method: Virtual reality-based two-way digital twin with a common HMI for programming all robots in a manufacturing cell, implemented as a cyber-physical system.
extractions.finding: VR-based digital twin enables simulation, testing, and safety checks before program deployment, providing a common programming interface for diverse manufacturing equipment.

extractions.problem: Measuring the psychological effect of an art intervention (virtual window) on quality of life in hospitalized patients undergoing stem cell transplantation.
extractions.method: Prospective randomized trial (N=199) comparing art intervention group vs control, measuring anxiety, depression, and stress via HADS and Distress Thermometer.
extractions.finding: The Open Window intervention significantly reduced anxiety and depression before and after transplant and improved patients' reported experiences.
extractions.dataset: 199 stem cell transplant patients for hematological malignancy (96 intervention, 103 control)
extractions.result: Anxiety reduction: p=0.001 (pre-transplant), p=0.041 (day 7), p=0.035 (day 60); depression p=0.022

extractions.problem: Reducing deployment costs of small cells in dense traffic areas by using large antenna arrays at macrocells as an alternative to physical small cell deployment.
extractions.method: Virtual Small Cells (VSCs) created by activating multiple antenna elements at macrocells to form directive beams, replacing picocells.
extractions.finding: VSCs are reconfigurable to traffic fluctuation, increase throughput by 50% over macro-only deployment, and reduce OPEX/CAPEX by eliminating physical small cell infrastructure.
extractions.result: 50% throughput increase, reduced global network power consumption

extractions.problem: Reviewing the physiological functions of plant cell walls in growth and environmental responses.
extractions.method: Editorial/virtual issue introducing published articles on cell wall functions across embryogenesis, vegetative growth, reproductive growth, and environmental responses.
extractions.finding: Cell walls have multiple functions including determining cell shape/size, cell-cell adhesion, controlling differentiation and growth, and promoting stress tolerance.
extractions.dataset: Plant cell wall studies (Journal of Plant Research collection)

extractions.problem: Enabling cellular access multi-tenancy through small cell base station virtualization to handle growing mobile traffic and standard diversity.
extractions.method: Virtualized small cell base station with multiple isolated LTE PHY stacks running on a hypervisor on off-the-shelf x86 hardware with shared radio head.
extractions.finding: Multiple virtualized base stations achieve throughput equal or close to theoretical maximum, with full stack at the edge eliminating the need for low-latency high-throughput fronthaul required by C-RAN.
extractions.result: Throughput equal or close to theoretical maximum

extractions.problem: Integrating cell formation, scheduling, and facility layout decisions in virtual manufacturing cells with assembly aspects.
extractions.method: Mathematical model minimizing material handling costs and cycle time, solved with Population-based Simulated Annealing (PSA) combined with linear programming.
extractions.finding: PSA showed superior performance compared to CPLEX solver and standard simulated annealing for concurrent scheduling and layout optimization.
extractions.dataset: Manufacturing cell case study, literature benchmark instances
extractions.metric: Material handling costs, cycle time, computation time
extractions.result: PSA outperformed CPLEX and standard SA on benchmark instances

extractions.problem: Evaluating whether low-energy (40-keV) virtual monochromatic dual-energy CT images improve visualization of head and neck squamous cell carcinoma compared to single-energy CT.
extractions.method: Comparison of 60 dual-energy CT and 60 SECT scans with objective (absolute/relative attenuation) and subjective evaluation by 6 radiologists.
extractions.finding: 40-keV VMIs significantly improved tumor visibility both objectively and subjectively, with substantial to almost perfect interrater agreement.
extractions.dataset: 120 CT scans (60 DECT, 60 SECT) from 2 institutions, head and neck SCC patients
extractions.result: Higher attenuation on 40-keV VMIs (p<0.0001), κ=0.61-1.0 interrater agreement

extractions.problem: Developing a production engineering-oriented virtual factory using a planning cell-based approach for manufacturing systems design.
Designing a scalable routing algorithm for the mobile subsystem of next-generation tactical communications systems.
extractions.method: Virtual cell layout (VCL) based dynamic source routing (VB-DSR) combining ad hoc and cellular infrastructureless architecture for tactical communications.
extractions.finding: VB-DSR is scalable for tactical communications, enabling 3G personal communications services through a hybrid ad hoc/cellular architecture.
extractions.result: Scalable performance for tactical communications

extractions.problem: Helping learners visualize the human cell division process (mitosis and meiosis) which is difficult to understand from traditional teaching methods.
extractions.method: Interactive mobile VR application (InCell VR) developed in Unity with mini games, evaluated via pre/post-tests and usability study.
extractions.finding: Students using the VR application showed positive knowledge gains and reported the app as useful, easy to use, and satisfying.
extractions.result: Positive knowledge gains; high usability ratings

extractions.problem: Building and testing complex hypotheses about intracellular processes by applying disciplined computational approaches that explicitly treat spatial complexity.
extractions.method: Review of computational cell biology tools with emphasis on Virtual Cell software, covering deterministic and stochastic simulations of spatiotemporal cellular events.
extractions.finding: General-purpose computational tools like Virtual Cell enable cell biologists to build spatial models and generate simulations, with applications demonstrated for RNA trafficking and neuronal calcium dynamics.
extractions.dataset: RNA trafficking, neuronal calcium dynamics (illustrative examples)

extractions.problem: Understanding how the convergence of mobile communications and computing drives social transformation.
extractions.method: Sociological and anthropological analysis of emerging mobile technology adoption across global cities (Tokyo, Helsinki, Manhattan, Manila).
extractions.finding: Mobile communications enable fundamentally new forms of collective action ('smart mobs'), with implications ranging from political activism to privacy threats.

extractions.problem: Investigating whether diatom frustules (silicified cell walls) provide mechanical protection and contribute to evolutionary success.
extractions.method: Real and virtual loading tests using calibrated glass microneedles and finite element analysis on centric and pennate diatom cells.
extractions.finding: Diatom frustules are remarkably strong due to their architecture and silica material properties, concluding they evolved as mechanical armor against predators.
extractions.result: Exceptional force required to break frustules

extractions.problem: Reviewing the potential of Punica granatum (pomegranate) for prevention and treatment of inflammation and cancer.
extractions.method: Comprehensive literature review of pharmacological activities across all pomegranate anatomical compartments (seed, juice, peel, leaf, flower, bark, roots).
extractions.finding: Pomegranate components possess antioxidant, weakly estrogenic, and anticancer activities including interference with tumor cell proliferation, cell cycle, invasion, and angiogenesis, associated with anti-inflammatory effects.
extractions.dataset: Punica granatum (pomegranate) phytochemical and pharmacological literature
extractions.result: 7x increase in Medline publications over prior period

extractions.problem: Comparing electronic structure calculation approaches for doped BaFe2As2 iron-based superconductor materials.
extractions.baseline: Virtual crystal approximation vs super-cell approach

extractions.problem: Deriving a more accurate equation for calculating interparticle spacing (IPS) in colloidal systems, which is critical for estimating suspension viscosity.
extractions.method: Mathematical derivation based on virtual cell concept (free volume per particle, originated by Kuwabara), validated against experimental titania data.
extractions.finding: The new IPS equation gives reasonable values including cases where the Dinger-Funk equation produced anomalous results.
extractions.metric: IPS values, comparison with experimental data
extractions.result: Reasonable IPS values where Dinger-Funk gave anomalous results

extractions.problem: Discovering small-molecule inhibitors of immune cell checkpoints as alternatives to monoclonal antibodies for immunotherapy across cancers and other diseases.
extractions.method: High-throughput virtual screening of 9,466 compounds against 7 immune checkpoints using docking, MM-GBSA, ADME filtering, and MD simulation.
extractions.finding: Rutin hydrate (targeting all 7 ICPs), amikacin hydrate (6 ICPs), and 6-hydroxyluteolin (3 ICPs) were identified as best immune checkpoint inhibitors.
extractions.dataset: 3 small-molecule libraries (9,466 compounds), 7 immune cell checkpoints
extractions.metric: Docking score, MM-GBSA, ADME properties, MD simulation stability
extractions.result: 13 ICP inhibitors identified; top 3 compounds targeting 3-7 checkpoints each

extractions.problem: Developing methodology for preventive ergonomic analysis of assembly work-cells in virtual environments before physical implementation.
extractions.method: Digital Human Models (DHM) and Virtual Reality tools for simulating manual assembly tasks, with adapted ergonomics evaluation methods for virtual environments.
extractions.finding: Virtual simulation with digital manikins enables early-stage ergonomic and feasibility analysis, suggesting solutions for more ergonomic and efficient assembly tasks.
extractions.result: New solutions identified for more ergonomic and efficient tasks

extractions.problem: Dynamically selecting UE-based virtual small cell base stations to handle non-stationary, non-uniform mobile traffic distribution in 5G networks.
extractions.method: Affinity Propagation Clustering to select optimal UE-VBSs, with modified message passing using received power as parameter; evaluated in MATLAB and NS3.
extractions.finding: The technique effectively selects UE-VBSs to supplement loaded SBSs, though scalability limitations are identified and addressed through modified message passing.
extractions.baseline: Static small cell deployment (Femto, Pico, Nano cells)

extractions.problem: Comparing target localization accuracy between conventional X-ray simulation and CT-based virtual simulation for palliative radiotherapy in non-small-cell lung cancer.
extractions.method: Randomized double-blind trial (N=86) comparing conventional simulator fields with digitally reconstructed radiographs from CT data.
extractions.finding: CT-based virtual simulation allows more accurate target definition, reducing geographical misses and treatment-related toxicity.
extractions.metric: Field area mismatch, target coverage, normal tissue over-coverage
extractions.result: Major mismatch in 66.2%; conventional fields 24.5% larger; target under-coverage 16.4%; normal tissue over-coverage 25.4%

extractions.problem: Developing InGaP single-junction solar cells on Ge-on-Si virtual substrates for cost-effective solar power conversion.
extractions.method: MOCVD growth on lattice-matched Ge-on-Si virtual substrates, optoelectronic simulation, and EBIC measurements of threading dislocation density.
extractions.finding: InGaP solar cells on Ge-on-Si virtual substrates achieve comparable Voc to those on Ge substrates when TDD < 2×10⁶ cm⁻², with estimated efficiencies up to 34.3%.
extractions.dataset: InGaP solar cells on Ge-on-Si and Ge substrates
extractions.baseline: InGaP solar cells on bulk Ge substrate
extractions.result: Voc 0.96-1.43 V at ~0.5 Sun; efficiency 16.6%-34.3% for 1.9-2.4 eV spectrum

extractions.problem: Integrating AI technologies (large language models, virtual cell simulations) to decipher complex multi-target mechanisms of Traditional Chinese Medicine for stroke treatment.
extractions.method: Review proposing theoretical frameworks combining AI with TCM research for both basic and clinical studies.
extractions.finding: AI can overcome traditional limitations in TCM research, enabling more precise mechanistic understanding and TCM-based stroke treatments.

extractions.problem: Scheduling jobs in virtual manufacturing cells with lot-streaming to minimize makespan, considering machine assignments, starting times, and sub-lot sizes.
extractions.method: Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) formulation with a Genetic Algorithm (GA) using a novel chromosome structure for VMC environments.
extractions.finding: The GA obtains optimal solutions when sub-lot number equals 1 and performs favorably across a wide range of problem instances.
extractions.result: GA obtains optimum for all instances with sub-lot number=1

extractions.problem: Creating a fine virtual electrode using electron beam for mechanical strain microscopy of living cells.
Visualizing complex protein interaction networks, complexes, and 3D structures in a biologically meaningful cellular context.
extractions.method: The Interactorium tool uses a Virtual Cell as context for seamless visualization, with algorithms to reduce complexity via putative complex generation and protein 'twins' for multi-location proteins.
extractions.finding: The tool can display networks of up to 40,000 proteins or 6,000 complexes with real-time data manipulation, enabling multi-level viewing of cellular molecular biology.
extractions.result: Handles 40,000 proteins or 6,000 complexes with real-time manipulation

extractions.problem: Developing maskless fabrication of light-directed oligonucleotide microarrays to replace costly photolithographic masks.
extractions.method: Maskless array synthesizer (MAS) using digital micromirror array to generate virtual masks with 1:1 reflective UV imaging on glass substrate in a flow cell reaction chamber.
extractions.finding: MAS synthesized microarrays with >76,000 features (16 μm²) at high yield, readily discriminating single-base pair mismatches after hybridization.
extractions.metric: Feature count, feature size, repetitive yield, single-base mismatch discrimination
extractions.result: >76,000 features at 16 μm², high repetitive yield, single-bp mismatch discrimination

extractions.problem: Mapping the optoelectronic property space of organic dyes and screening for dyes suitable for dye-sensitized water splitting and solar cells.
extractions.method: High-throughput virtual screening of 45,000 diketopyrrolopyrrole (DPP) dyes for optoelectronic properties.
extractions.finding: Property space mapping identified DPP dyes suitable for dye-sensitized proton reduction and dye-sensitized solar cells.
extractions.result: 45,000 dyes screened

extractions.problem: Rapidly and cheaply prototyping and optimizing novel nano-scale solar cell designs through computational simulation.
extractions.method: FDTD simulations coupled with particle swarm optimization (PSO) for thin film silicon plasmonic and photonic crystal organic solar cells.
extractions.finding: Optical enhancements of approximately 15% can be achieved with optimization requiring only a few hundred simulations (hours on a workstation).
extractions.dataset: Thin film silicon plasmonic solar cells, photonic crystal organic solar cells
extractions.result: ~15% optical enhancement, few hundred simulations in a few hours

extractions.problem: Developing a tractable analytical model for virtualized downlink cellular networks with ultra-dense small cells using C-plane/U-plane splitting.
extractions.method: Stochastic geometry-based analytical framework for modeling virtualized RAN with control/user plane separation.
extractions.finding: The model provides design insights for virtualized RANs and quantifies performance improvement from RAN virtualization in ultra-dense deployments.
extractions.metric: SINR, spatial spectral efficiency, network throughput

extractions.problem: Comparing learning gains and self-efficacy between traditional live laboratory and virtual computer simulation experiences for cell and molecular biology concepts.
extractions.method: Laboratory module using S. cerevisiae mating pathway with live experiments and Unity-based computer simulations; assessed via pre/post knowledge tests and attitudinal surveys.
extractions.finding: Students performing simulation alone or simulation plus live lab demonstrated learning gains (greater for live lab), while students performing neither did not; both modalities increased engagement and self-efficacy.
extractions.dataset: Undergraduate biology students, Saccharomyces cerevisiae mating pathway
extractions.result: Significant learning gains for simulation and live lab groups; greater gains for live lab

extractions.problem: Visualizing mitochondria in living cells without fluorescent markers using computational approaches.
extractions.method: Physics-guided deep learning with adversarial neural network integrating microscope point spread function for virtual labeling from bright-field images.
extractions.finding: The approach achieves significantly better virtual labeling of mitochondria than state-of-the-art, enabling segmentation and tracking previously possible only with fluorescent labels.
extractions.dataset: Living cell bright-field and fluorescence microscopy images
extractions.result: Average Pearson correlation 0.86 vs SOTA 0.71

extractions.problem: Designing an interactive virtual environment for modeling factories using cellular manufacturing methodologies.
extractions.method: Open-architecture virtual factory with Modified Boolean Method clustering algorithm for automatic cell formation and 3D visualization.
extractions.finding: The virtual factory makes cellular manufacturing design intuitive, enabling better optimization for maintainability and performance that would not be visible without 3D visualization.
extractions.result: Automatic generation of complex virtual manufacturing environments

extractions.problem: Investigating the in vivo function of BMP-7 using gene knockout in mice.
extractions.method: Generation of BMP-7-deficient mice using embryonic stem cell technology, with histological analysis and in situ hybridization at multiple developmental stages.
extractions.finding: BMP-7 is essential for kidney development (nephrogenesis inducer) — deficient mice show virtual absence of glomeruli — and also required for eye development and skeletal patterning.
extractions.metric: Histological analysis, Pax-2 and Wnt-4 expression
extractions.result: Virtual absence of glomeruli in newborn kidneys; affected Pax-2/Wnt-4 expression at 12.5-14.5 dpc

extractions.problem: Designing virtual manufacturing cells with optimized machine sharing to improve utilization and respond to demand fluctuations.
extractions.method: Two-stage mathematical approach: Stage 1 optimizes number of part families, Stage 2 uses novel formulation with new variables defining shared machines in virtual cells.
extractions.finding: The proposed method accurately defines shared machine numbers and was validated in a real plant, demonstrating practicability.
extractions.metric: Machine utilization, number of shared machines
extractions.result: Validated in plant application; improved accuracy of shared machine definition

extractions.problem: Understanding how multisensory cues beyond vision and self-motion contribute to hippocampal place cell spatial coding.
extractions.method: Comparison of hippocampal place cell activity in rats running in immersive virtual reality versus real-world environments.
extractions.finding: Twice as many neurons were active in the real world compared to VR; place cells encoded position in the real world but encoded distance in virtual reality.
extractions.dataset: Rat hippocampal place cells, virtual reality vs real-world navigation
extractions.metric: Number of active neurons, spatial vs distance encoding
extractions.result: 2x more active neurons in real world; VR place cells encode distance instead of position

extractions.problem: Identifying security challenges of small cell as a service in virtualized mobile edge computing environments.
Minimizing energy consumption of energy-harvesting virtual small cells by dynamically controlling functional splits between local processing and edge cloud.
extractions.method: Multi-agent reinforcement learning (distributed Fuzzy Q-Learning and Q-Learning) for dynamic functional split control in Cloud RAN architecture.
extractions.finding: Coordinated multi-agent RL via broadcasting system-level information achieves higher cumulative rewards closer to offline bounds than uncoordinated solutions, with continuous state/action enabling faster convergence.
extractions.dataset: Simulated dense small cell network with energy harvesters
extractions.metric: Energy minimization, cumulative reward, convergence speed
extractions.baseline: Off-line optimization bounds, uncoordinated learning agents
extractions.result: Coordinated agents achieve rewards closer to off-line bounds; faster convergence with continuous representation

extractions.problem: Designing an effective emergency broadcast protocol for sensor-based inter-vehicle communications on highways.
extractions.method: Geographical routing-based protocol dividing highway into virtual cells with dynamically elected cell reflectors for message prioritization and forwarding; evaluated by simulation.
extractions.finding: The proposed protocol is more effective than existing inter-vehicle protocols for emergency message dissemination.
extractions.result: More effective than existing protocols

extractions.problem: Incorporating spatial features (cell shape, compartment sizes, molecule distribution) into mechanistic mathematical models of cell signaling.
extractions.method: Virtual Cell software for spatial modeling with deterministic and stochastic formulations; reaction-diffusion numerical methods with geometry specification.
extractions.finding: Spatial modeling provides insights for interpreting experimental results, demonstrated through cAMP signaling in neurons.

extractions.problem: Improving mortality prognostication for critically ill pediatric allogeneic hematopoietic cell transplant patients requiring PICU admission.
extractions.method: Merged Virtual Pediatric Systems and CIBMTR databases; evaluated 25 early risk factors using multivariable modeling for PICU mortality prediction in 936 patients.
extractions.finding: Five independent risk factors were identified; top-quartile risk patients had 3x greater mortality than others, with classification accuracy of 75.2%.
extractions.dataset: 936 critically ill pediatric allogeneic HCT patients, 1,532 PICU admissions (2009-2014)
extractions.metric: PICU mortality rate, classification accuracy, 95% CI
extractions.result: Overall PICU mortality 17.4%; top quartile 35.1% vs 11.5% (p<0.001); classification accuracy 75.2%

extractions.problem: Developing a method for automated drivability calibration earlier in vehicle development without requiring mature hardware.
extractions.method: Model-based calibration using real-time physics-based engine/vehicle simulation with real engine controller hardware in a virtual engine test cell, combined with online optimization.
extractions.finding: Automated drivability calibration in a virtual test cell environment is feasible, with accurate engine simulation under steady-state and transient conditions sensitive to calibration changes.
extractions.dataset: Engine simulation model (steady-state and transient conditions)
extractions.metric: Simulation accuracy, sensitivity to calibration changes
extractions.baseline: Traditional vehicle drivability calibration on mature hardware
extractions.result: Feasible automated calibration; compresses concept-to-production timeline
